calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Calcium binds to levothyroxine in the GI tract, reducing absorption by up to 30%.

iron_sulfate.mechanism: Iron forms insoluble complexes with levothyroxine, dramatically reducing bioavailability.

aluminum_hydroxide.mechanism: Antacids containing aluminum bind thyroid hormones in the gut.

omeprazole.mechanism: PPIs reduce gastric acidity needed for levothyroxine dissolution and absorption.

coffee.mechanism: Coffee reduces levothyroxine absorption by up to 36% when taken concurrently.
coffee.resolution: Wait at least 60 minutes after taking levothyroxine before drinking coffee.

soy.mechanism: Soy protein interferes with levothyroxine absorption in the intestine.
soy.resolution: Maintain consistent soy intake; take levothyroxine 4 hours apart.

magnesium_oxide.mechanism: Magnesium forms insoluble chelate complexes with levothyroxine.

soy_milk.mechanism: Soy protein can bind thyroid hormone in the gut, reducing absorption.
soy_milk.resolution: Take levothyroxine at least 4 hours apart from soy containing foods.

aspirin.mechanism: Combined anticoagulant and antiplatelet effects dramatically increase hemorrhage risk.
aspirin.resolution: Avoid concurrent use unless under strict medical supervision.

ibuprofen.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit platelet function and may displace warfarin from protein binding sites.
ibuprofen.resolution: Avoid NSAIDs with warfarin; use acetaminophen for pain.

vitamin_k.mechanism: Vitamin K directly antagonizes warfarin's anticoagulant mechanism.
vitamin_k.resolution: Maintain consistent vitamin K intake; do not supplement without medical guidance.

cranberry.mechanism: Cranberry juice may inhibit CYP2C9, increasing warfarin levels and bleeding risk.
cranberry.resolution: Limit cranberry intake; monitor INR closely.

ginger.mechanism: Ginger has mild antiplatelet properties that may enhance warfarin's effect.
ginger.resolution: Use ginger in moderation; monitor INR.

grapefruit.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4 and may alter warfarin metabolism.
grapefruit.resolution: Avoid grapefruit or maintain very consistent intake.

green_tea.mechanism: Green tea contains vitamin K which can reduce warfarin effectiveness.
green_tea.resolution: Limit green tea to consistent moderate amounts.

ginkgo_biloba.mechanism: Ginkgolides have antiplatelet activity; combination with warfarin increases bleeding risk without altering INR.
ginkgo_biloba.resolution: Avoid ginkgo supplements while on anticoagulants.

st_johns_wort.mechanism: St. John's Wort strongly induces CYP2C9 and CYP3A4, dramatically reducing warfarin levels and risking blood clots.
st_johns_wort.resolution: Avoid St. John's Wort entirely. Monitor INR.

garlic_extract_allicin.mechanism: Garlic has mild antiplatelet effects and can increase bleeding risk when taken with warfarin.

ginseng.mechanism: Ginseng may decrease warfarin effectiveness, lowering INR and increasing clotting risk.

spinach.mechanism: Spinach is rich in Vitamin K, which directly counteracts warfarin's anticoagulant mechanism, lowering INR and increasing clot risk.
spinach.resolution: Maintain a highly consistent daily intake of Vitamin K foods; do not make sudden dietary changes.

coenzyme_q10_ubiquinol.mechanism: CoQ10 is structurally similar to Vitamin K and may reduce the anticoagulant effect of warfarin.
coenzyme_q10_ubiquinol.resolution: Avoid CoQ10 supplements or monitor INR closely.

green_tea_extract_egcg.mechanism: Green tea contains Vitamin K which can antagonize warfarin's action.

alcohol.mechanism: Alcohol impairs hepatic gluconeogenesis; combined with metformin increases lactic acidosis risk.
alcohol.resolution: Limit alcohol significantly or avoid entirely while on metformin.

iodinated_contrast.mechanism: Contrast media with metformin increases risk of contrast-induced nephropathy and lactic acidosis.
iodinated_contrast.resolution: Stop metformin 48 hours before and after contrast procedures.

beverage_alcoholic.mechanism: Alcohol enhances the effect of metformin on lactate metabolism, dramatically increasing the risk of potentially fatal lactic acidosis.
beverage_alcoholic.resolution: Avoid alcohol completely while taking metformin.

calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Divalent cations chelate fluoroquinolones, reducing antibiotic absorption by up to 90%.
calcium_carbonate.resolution: Take ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after calcium.

iron_sulfate.mechanism: Iron chelates fluoroquinolones, rendering the antibiotic ineffective.
iron_sulfate.resolution: Separate by 2 hours before or 6 hours after.

magnesium_oxide.mechanism: Magnesium chelates ciprofloxacin, significantly reducing absorption.

dairy.mechanism: Dairy calcium chelates fluoroquinolones, reducing absorption.

caffeine.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2, increasing caffeine levels and causing jitteriness.
caffeine.resolution: Reduce caffeine intake while on ciprofloxacin.

calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Calcium chelates tetracycline, forming insoluble complexes.

dairy.mechanism: Dairy calcium forms insoluble chelates with tetracycline.

antacids.mechanism: Antacid metals (Al, Mg, Ca) all chelate tetracyclines.

grapefruit.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4, increasing amlodipine plasma levels and risk of hypotension.

simvastatin.mechanism: Amlodipine inhibits CYP3A4, increasing simvastatin levels and myopathy risk.
simvastatin.resolution: Limit simvastatin to 20mg/day when combined with amlodipine.

grapefruit.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4, significantly increasing statin blood levels and rhabdomyolysis risk.
grapefruit.resolution: Avoid grapefruit entirely while on atorvastatin.

erythromycin.mechanism: Erythromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4, dramatically increasing statin toxicity risk.
erythromycin.resolution: Use alternative antibiotic or temporarily hold statin.

gemfibrozil.mechanism: Gemfibrozil inhibits statin glucuronidation and OATP1B1 transport, causing severe myopathy.
gemfibrozil.resolution: Avoid combination; use fenofibrate instead if fibrate needed.

clopidogrel.mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, preventing conversion of clopidogrel to its active metabolite.
clopidogrel.resolution: Use pantoprazole instead, which has less CYP2C19 inhibition.

iron_sulfate.mechanism: PPIs reduce gastric acid needed for iron salt dissolution and absorption.
iron_sulfate.resolution: Use iron bisglycinate form or take iron during PPI 'off' periods.

calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Reduced stomach acid impairs calcium carbonate dissolution.
calcium_carbonate.resolution: Use calcium citrate instead, which doesn't require acid for absorption.

vitamin_b12.mechanism: Long-term PPI use reduces B12 absorption by decreasing intrinsic factor release.

magnesium_oxide.mechanism: PPIs reduce magnesium absorption with chronic use.
magnesium_oxide.resolution: Monitor magnesium levels; use magnesium glycinate.

potassium_chloride.mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone secretion, causing potassium retention; supplements compound this.

spironolactone.mechanism: Both drugs increase potassium; combination greatly increases hyperkalemia risk.
spironolactone.resolution: If combined, monitor potassium levels very closely.

ibuprofen.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce ACE inhibitor effectiveness and increase renal impairment risk.
ibuprofen.resolution: Use acetaminophen for pain; avoid chronic NSAID use.

ibuprofen.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit renal clearance of methotrexate, leading to severe bone marrow suppression and GI toxicity.
ibuprofen.resolution: Avoid NSAIDs; use acetaminophen for pain relief.

aspirin.mechanism: Aspirin displaces methotrexate from protein binding and reduces renal clearance.

folic_acid.mechanism: Folic acid may slightly reduce methotrexate efficacy but protects against toxicity.
folic_acid.resolution: Take folic acid as prescribed to reduce side effects.

alcohol.mechanism: Both are hepatotoxic; combination dramatically increases liver damage risk.
alcohol.resolution: Avoid alcohol completely while on methotrexate.

naproxen.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce methotrexate excretion, risking life-threatening toxicity.

omeprazole.mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits renal H+/K+-ATPase and organic anion transporters, reducing methotrexate elimination and risking toxicity.
omeprazole.resolution: Avoid PPIs during high-dose methotrexate therapy.

omeprazole_20mg.resolution: Monitor levels or use H2 blocker instead.

omeprazole_40mg.mechanism: High-dose PPI reduces methotrexate excretion, raising toxicity risk.

aspirin.mechanism: Ibuprofen blocks aspirin's antiplatelet binding site, reducing cardioprotection.
aspirin.resolution: Take aspirin 30 min before ibuprofen, or use alternative analgesic.

alcohol.mechanism: NSAIDs + alcohol synergistically damage gastric mucosa and increase GI bleeding risk.

prednisone.mechanism: Corticosteroids + NSAIDs greatly increase GI ulcer and perforation risk.
prednisone.resolution: Use gastroprotective agent (PPI) if combination is unavoidable.

lithium.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal lithium clearance, causing toxic lithium levels.
lithium.resolution: Avoid NSAIDs; monitor lithium levels if unavoidable.

calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Calcium inhibits non-heme iron absorption by up to 60%.
calcium_carbonate.resolution: Separate iron and calcium by at least 2 hours.

coffee.mechanism: Coffee polyphenols chelate iron, reducing absorption by up to 39%.
coffee.resolution: Avoid coffee 1 hour before and after iron.

tea.mechanism: Tea tannins bind iron, reducing absorption by up to 64%.
tea.resolution: Avoid tea 1 hour before and after iron supplements.

vitamin_c.mechanism: Vitamin C enhances non-heme iron absorption (this is beneficial).
vitamin_c.resolution: Take vitamin C with iron for better absorption.

zinc.mechanism: Zinc and iron compete for the same absorption transporter (DMT1).
zinc.resolution: Separate zinc and iron supplements by 2 hours.

zinc_picolinate.mechanism: High concentrations of iron can inhibit zinc absorption.

zinc.resolution: Take zinc and calcium at different times of day.

zinc_picolinate.mechanism: High doses of calcium can compete with zinc for absorption in the small intestine.

calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Calcium chelates doxycycline, reducing antibiotic efficacy significantly.

iron_sulfate.mechanism: Iron chelates doxycycline, forming insoluble complexes.

st_johns_wort.mechanism: St. John's Wort increases serotonin; combination risks serotonin syndrome.
st_johns_wort.resolution: Do NOT combine with any SSRI/SNRI antidepressant.

tramadol.mechanism: Tramadol has serotonergic activity; combined with SSRIs increases serotonin syndrome risk.
tramadol.resolution: Use alternative analgesic or monitor closely.

tryptophan.mechanism: Tryptophan is a serotonin precursor; supplementation with SSRIs may cause serotonin excess.
tryptophan.resolution: Avoid tryptophan supplements while on SSRIs.

calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Divalent calcium ions chelate alendronate, reducing its bone absorption by over 60%.
calcium_carbonate.resolution: Take alendronate 30 minutes before calcium or food in the morning.

coffee.resolution: Take alendronate with plain water only; wait 30 min before coffee.

coffee_black.mechanism: Coffee reduces the absorption of alendronate by up to 60%.
coffee_black.resolution: Take alendronate with plain water only, 30 minutes before coffee or food.

amiodarone.mechanism: Amiodarone increases digoxin levels by 70-100%, risking toxicity.
amiodarone.resolution: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone.

st_johns_wort.mechanism: St. John's Wort induces P-glycoprotein, reducing digoxin levels by 25%.
st_johns_wort.resolution: Avoid St. John's Wort; monitor digoxin levels.

fiber.mechanism: High fiber intake can reduce digoxin absorption.
fiber.resolution: Take digoxin separately from high-fiber meals.

calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Hypercalcemia increases the cardiotoxicity of digoxin, potentially causing cardiac arrhythmias.
calcium_carbonate.resolution: Avoid concurrent calcium supplements or monitor levels closely.

licorice_root.mechanism: Licorice causes potassium depletion (hypokalemia), which severely sensitizes the heart to digoxin toxicity.
licorice_root.resolution: Avoid licorice-containing foods/teas while taking digoxin.

fiber_psyllium.mechanism: Soluble fibers can bind digoxin in the GI tract, reducing its absorption.

tyramine.mechanism: MAOIs prevent tyramine metabolism; accumulated tyramine causes hypertensive crisis.
tyramine.resolution: Strictly avoid aged cheeses, cured meats, fermented foods, red wine.

nitroglycerin.mechanism: Nitrates combined with PDE5 inhibitors cause synergistic vasodilation leading to severe, potentially fatal hypotension.
nitroglycerin.resolution: Do NOT combine. Separate by at least 24-48 hours.

nitroglycerin.mechanism: Tadalafil has a long half-life; combination with nitrates causes life-threatening blood pressure drops.
nitroglycerin.resolution: Do NOT combine. Separate by at least 48 hours.

potassium_chloride.mechanism: Spironolactone is a potassium-sparing diuretic; concurrent potassium supplements can cause severe hyperkalemia.
potassium_chloride.resolution: Avoid potassium supplements. Monitor serum potassium.

enalapril.mechanism: Concurrent potassium-sparing diuretic and ACE inhibitor therapy increases risk of hyperkalemia.

fluoxetine.mechanism: Fluoxetine is a strong CYP2D6 inhibitor, blocking metoprolol metabolism and increasing cardiorespiratory side effects.
fluoxetine.resolution: Monitor heart rate and blood pressure; consider lower metoprolol dose.

fluoxetine.mechanism: CYP2D6 inhibition by fluoxetine increases carvedilol levels, worsening bradycardia.

grapefruit.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4, increasing cyclosporine blood levels by up to 300%, leading to nephrotoxicity.
grapefruit.resolution: Avoid grapefruit and grapefruit juice entirely.

st_johns_wort.mechanism: St. John's Wort induces CYP3A4, dropping cyclosporine levels and causing organ transplant rejection.

tramadol.mechanism: Both drugs increase synaptic serotonin levels; combination raises the risk of Serotonin Syndrome.
tramadol.resolution: Avoid combination or monitor closely for tremors, rigidity, and hyperthermia.

st_johns_wort.mechanism: Combined SSRI and St. John's Wort causes additive serotonergic toxicity.
st_johns_wort.resolution: Do not supplement St. John's Wort while on antidepressants.

ibuprofen.mechanism: SSRIs inhibit platelet serotonin uptake; combined with NSAIDs greatly increases upper GI bleeding risk.
ibuprofen.resolution: Monitor for dark stools; use gastroprotection.

tramadol.mechanism: Additive serotonergic pathways increase risk of Serotonin Syndrome and seizures.

st_johns_wort.mechanism: SSRI + St. John's Wort triggers serotonin syndrome.

ibuprofen.mechanism: SSRI + NSAID additive antiplatelet effects raise bleed risk.
ibuprofen.resolution: Avoid combination or use with care.

sertraline.mechanism: MAO inhibitors combined with SSRIs block serotonin degradation, causing fatal serotonin syndrome.
sertraline.resolution: Requires at least 14 days washout period between these medications.

iron_sulfate.mechanism: Iron chelates levodopa in the GI tract, reducing its absorption and therapeutic effect in Parkinson's.
iron_sulfate.resolution: Separate doses by at least 2 hours.

vitamin_b6_pyridoxine_hcl.mechanism: Pyridoxine accelerates peripheral decarboxylation of levodopa, reducing the amount reaching the brain.
vitamin_b6_pyridoxine_hcl.resolution: Avoid B6 doses > 5mg/day unless taking carbidopa.

beef_steak.mechanism: Dietary amino acids compete with levodopa for transport across the intestinal wall and blood-brain barrier.
beef_steak.resolution: Take levodopa 30-60 minutes before protein-rich meals.

ibuprofen.mechanism: NSAIDs decrease renal lithium clearance, raising serum lithium levels and risking toxicity.
ibuprofen.resolution: Monitor lithium levels; avoid NSAIDs if possible.

naproxen.mechanism: NSAID-induced prostaglandin inhibition reduces renal blood flow, increasing lithium levels.

coffee_black.mechanism: Caffeine increases renal lithium excretion; sudden caffeine withdrawal can spike lithium levels.

calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Calcium reduces phenytoin absorption and blood levels, risking seizures.
calcium_carbonate.resolution: Separate dose by at least 2 hours.

vitamin_b9_folic_acid.mechanism: Folic acid acts as a cofactor in phenytoin metabolism, increasing its clearance and lowering blood levels, potentially triggering seizures.
vitamin_b9_folic_acid.resolution: Monitor phenytoin levels if supplementing folic acid.

grapefruit.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4, increasing carbamazepine levels and causing ataxia, nystagmus, and dizziness.

st_johns_wort.mechanism: St. John's Wort induces CYP3A4, increasing carbamazepine metabolism and decreasing blood levels, risking seizures.
St. John's Wort induces CYP3A4, accelerating contraceptive estrogen metabolism and risking breakthrough bleeding and pregnancy.
st_johns_wort.resolution: Use barrier contraception; avoid St. John's Wort.

grapefruit.mechanism: Grapefruit increases tacrolimus levels via CYP3A4 inhibition, causing severe nephrotoxicity.
Grapefruit increases verapamil exposure, increasing risk of bradycardia and hypotension.
Grapefruit increases buspirone levels, causing dizziness and drowsiness.
Grapefruit CYP3A4 inhibition increases quetiapine exposure and somnolence.
CYP3A4 inhibition by grapefruit significantly increases antipsychotic levels and side effects.

beverage_alcoholic.mechanism: Alcohol combined with high dose metformin increases risk of lactic acidosis.

grapefruit.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition by grapefruit increases concentration of high-dose atorvastatin, raising risk of muscle breakdown.
Grapefruit increases amlodipine concentration, boosting risk of ankle swelling and low blood pressure.

amiodarone.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4 metabolism of simvastatin. Concomitant use increases risk of rhabdomyolysis (simvastatin dose should not exceed 20mg/day).
amiodarone.resolution: Avoid combination or limit simvastatin dose to 20mg/day maximum.

amiodarone_200mg.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition increases simvastatin levels, causing muscle toxicity.

spinach.mechanism: Vitamin K in spinach opposes warfarin action.
High Vitamin K foods antagonize anticoagulation.

beverage_alcoholic.mechanism: Chronic alcohol consumption induces CYP2E1, forming a hepatotoxic metabolite (NAPQI) from acetaminophen, increasing liver failure risk.
beverage_alcoholic.resolution: Avoid combining alcohol and acetaminophen. Limit acetaminophen to 2000mg/day if drinking.

beverage_alcoholic.mechanism: Alcohol increases acetaminophen liver toxicity via glutathione depletion.

calcium_carbonate.mechanism: Calcium chelates fluoroquinolone antibiotics in the stomach.
calcium_carbonate.resolution: Separate by 2 hours before or 6 hours after.

calcium_citrate.mechanism: Calcium chelates ciprofloxacin, reducing antibiotic efficacy.

tamoxifen.mechanism: Fluoxetine is a potent CYP2D6 inhibitor, preventing conversion of tamoxifen to its active metabolite endoxifen, reducing cancer therapy efficacy.
tamoxifen.resolution: Avoid fluoxetine; use an alternative antidepressant like escitalopram.

tamoxifen.mechanism: CYP2D6 inhibition blocks activation of tamoxifen.

magnesium_oxide.mechanism: Magnesium antacids reduce the absorption of gabapentin by approximately 20%.
magnesium_oxide.resolution: Take gabapentin at least 2 hours after magnesium supplements.

magnesium_glycinate.mechanism: Magnesium glycinate may slightly reduce gabapentin absorption.

spironolactone_50mg.resolution: Avoid potassium-sparing combinations or monitor closely.

omeprazole_20mg.resolution: Do not take omeprazole with clopidogrel.

sertraline.mechanism: Linezolid is a weak, reversible MAOI. Combined with SSRIs, it can cause severe, potentially fatal Serotonin Syndrome.
sertraline.resolution: Do NOT combine. Stop sertraline at least 14 days before starting linezolid.

escitalopram.mechanism: MAOI antibiotic combined with SSRI risks serotonin syndrome.

dextromethorphan.mechanism: Combining MAOI antibiotic with serotonergic cough suppressant can trigger Serotonin Syndrome.

sertraline.mechanism: Dextromethorphan has mild serotonergic properties; combining with high SSRI doses risks Serotonin Syndrome.
sertraline.resolution: Use caution; avoid high doses of cough medicine.

coffee_black.mechanism: Combined CNS stimulants can cause additive heart rate elevation, palpitations, jitteriness, and insomnia.
coffee_black.resolution: Avoid caffeine and high coffee intake while on decongestants.

green_tea_extract_egcg.mechanism: Additive stimulant effects may cause tachycardia.

beverage_alcoholic.mechanism: Alcohol can increase propranolol absorption and plasma levels while adding to CNS depressant effects.
Alcohol increases propranolol levels and lowers blood pressure.

coffee_black.mechanism: Caffeine inhibits CYP1A2, which metabolizes melatonin, and acts as a central stimulant, blocking melatonin's sleep onset benefits.
coffee_black.resolution: Do not consume caffeine near bedtime.

alprazolam.mechanism: Kava kava has sedative properties and can act synergistically with benzodiazepines, risking severe somnolence, motor impairment, and lethargy.

beverage_alcoholic.mechanism: Valerian increases GABA activity; combining with alcohol causes additive sedation and psychomotor impairment.
beverage_alcoholic.resolution: Avoid alcohol when taking valerian root.

ibuprofen.mechanism: Ginkgo inhibits platelet aggregation; combined with NSAIDs like ibuprofen, the risk of GI bleeding is elevated.
ibuprofen.resolution: Avoid NSAIDs with ginkgo; use acetaminophen.

aspirin.mechanism: Additive antiplatelet effects increase microbleeding and bruising risk.

cyclosporine.mechanism: Echinacea stimulates immune function and can counteract the action of immunosuppressant drugs.

atorvastatin.mechanism: Red yeast rice naturally contains monacolin K (lovastatin); combining with prescription statins increases risk of muscle toxicity.
atorvastatin.resolution: Do not combine red yeast rice with prescription statins.

simvastatin.mechanism: Additive statin exposure increases risk of myopathy.

clarithromycin.mechanism: Clarithromycin is a strong CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein inhibitor, significantly raising colchicine exposure and risking fatal toxicity.

amoxicillin.mechanism: Concurrent use increases the incidence of drug-induced skin rash.
amoxicillin.resolution: Monitor for skin rashes; report changes immediately.
